supportive and friendly environment. The team is like family, and management genuinely cares about employees' well-being. There are also opportunities for advancement and flexible scheduling. Great workplace with a fun team!

Competitive pay for food service, flexible scheduling, strong training program, and clear standards. Team environment is supportive, and there are opportunities for advancement if you work hard. Great team environment but physically demanding
Lots of friendly coworkers, recognition for good work and cool managers, lots of feedback from managers and higher level employees Great work environment

Compared to other fast food jobs the pay and work environment are great. The company culture is very welcoming and supportive, and there is definitely room for growth if it's a career path you want to take. Good Money Tough Job
Good work environment. Enjoyed my management Good work environment

evidence.role: Current employee - Level III In N Out Associate
evidence.text: Stressful environment, management doesnât get involved in when associates are facing harassment from customers and coworkers Stressful job bad management
Slightly unfair at times, can be very stressful and sometimes overwhelming, relationships matter. Stressful Workplace
can be stressful, also long hours mmmm in n out

The quality of your shift will depend on which manager is leading, and can be completely unpredictable. Depending on your store location, you can have practically no business or never-ending orders all day. The work is tiring, especially the grill, and you're expected to just be able to handle it, and are often not given your break on time. The company doesn't care about work-life balance and expects you to regularl...

-Very fast pace (at least for me) -Not much downtime or breathing room -Requires high energy and a lot of social battery -Strict dress-code standards (But that comes naturally with specifically In-N-Out, not a massive massive issue) Not for me, but still a very positive experience!
Little support from shift leaders and managers. No work life balance. Okay experience
The umbrellas weren't that great for heat protection Good pay flexible hours
Always on your feet, tiring long days but worth it. Great place to work as a first job
Toxic management Lots of favoritism Shady reporting Lots of Favoritism

This is the most toxic environment I have ever worked in. Management allows and participates in bullying and HR blatantly disregards complaints and concerns. I have witnessed almost every type of bigotry in the year and a half that I worked there. Very Toxic in Every Way

evidence.role: Current employee - In N Out Associate level 4
evidence.text: toxic environment, managers hook up with their employees, favoritism, nepotism, manager favorites, sucks when you aren't one. so much drama and many friend groups that exclude others. been there 3 years and people hired a year after me moved up to cook as managers favorite. its a 50/50, good starter job

Really hard to get full time Late policy is incredibly strict. Had multiple write ups while I was there for being 1-5 min late. The computers flag you INSTANTLY SUPER militant. Your uniform has to be perfect, the product has to be perfect, your customer service has to be perfect. YOU have to be perfect No dyed hair, men also have to be kept clean shaven & have to have short hair. No visible tattoos, you just hav...
-Some favoritism, some people may progress faster than you, even if hired later My Experience
- HORRIBLE abusive leadership - Leadership doesnt even know how to do their own subordinates job - promotions happen only amongst friends. like seeeestars HORRIBLE INCOMPTENT LEADERSHIP

toxic work environment, stressful, unrewarding, exhausting - overall just a dead end job with very little at stake but a very high investment from the people who get stuck there dead end retail job with decent pay and toxic work culture
- Demanding work - Strict grooming standards for both men and women - Can be a dead end job Good Company but demanding
Didn't know they're a religious company good for high school students
If you do not speak up and seek training, you may sometimes be overlooked. A Great Place to Work
Inconsistent hours, lower levels won't get many hours. Nice Work Environment

Gossip and drama is very prevalent and management doesn't always take your issues seriously. Can be irritating to go into work knowing that's what's waiting for you Great for money and experience; a lot of gossip

Warehouse/ drivers only paid well not treated as good as store associates. Upper management doesn't listen to us on ground only what looks good on paper. Careless for utah support side Great place if your not on support side
stressful & chaotic, certain management is not nice A good job for teenagers/college students
The fast pace might not be for everyone Great place to work
